description:
You are mentoring a junior machine-learning engineer who needs to learn a few essential
    process-management skills while preparing training data.  
    Walk them through the following workflow **using no more than about 5-10 shell
    commands in total** (not counting small helpers such as `export` or variable
    assignments).

    Workflow goals
    1. Create the directory  /home/user/ml_project/preprocessing  if it does not already exist.
    2. Launch **one** long-running background process that pretends to “prepare” data.
       • Use the simplest possible command – e.g. `sleep 600` – so that it stays alive for at least ten minutes.  
       • Make sure the command *really* ends up running in the background (i.e. the shell prompt must return immediately).  
       • Capture its PID in a shell variable because you will need it again.
    3. Verify the process’ current nice value (priority) and record it.
    4. Change the process’ nice value to **10** (i.e. make it run with lower priority).
    5. Verify the new nice value and record it again.
    6. Save both nice values to a small log file so that an automated grading script can
       check your work.
    7. Also save the PID to a separate file so that the grader can clean up the process
       afterwards.

    File & directory requirements
    After the workflow finishes, **all three** of the following items must exist exactly
    as specified below.

    A. Directory  
       /home/user/ml_project/preprocessing

    B. Nice-history log  
       Path:  /home/user/ml_project/preprocessing/nice_history.log  
       Format (must match *exactly*):  
           Line 1  →  0  
           Line 2  →  10  
       • Each line is the integer alone, followed by a single UNIX line-feed (`\n`).  
       • No leading or trailing spaces, no extra blank lines, no header text.

    C. PID file  
       Path:  /home/user/ml_project/preprocessing/pipeline.pid  
       Format: a single decimal integer (the PID of the background `sleep 600`
       process) followed by a line-feed.  No other text.

    Runtime requirement  
    • When you finish, the `sleep 600` process whose PID you stored in
      pipeline.pid must still be running **and** have a nice value of 10.

    Reminder: you do **not** need root privileges for any step.  
    Keep the whole solution to roughly 5-10 significant shell commands.  
    Be sure each file matches the exact formats above so the automated tests can
    validate your work.
truth:
Files/directories that must exist after the agent completes:

    1. Directory: /home/user/ml_project/preprocessing
       – Must be present.

    2. File: /home/user/ml_project/preprocessing/nice_history.log  
       – Exact contents (two lines, each terminated with LF):
         0\n
         10\n

    3. File: /home/user/ml_project/preprocessing/pipeline.pid  
       – Contents: a single positive integer followed by LF.  
       – The integer must correspond to a running process whose command line is exactly
         "sleep 600" and whose current nice value is 10.

    The grading script will:
      • Verify the directory exists.  
      • Read nice_history.log and require the exact byte sequence "0\n10\n".  
      • Read pipeline.pid, parse the integer PID, then use `ps -p <PID> -o ni=,args=` to
        confirm the process is alive, is "sleep 600", and has nice value 10.

    Any deviation from the above will be marked incorrect.
name: task_000000_ee4bfeeb
